summary: aica worker turns installer's host into a remote-controlled agent with full file/command access
details: When `aica start` is run, the package launches long-poll workers that connect to the hardcoded server https://aca.kinghon.com.cn:8843/ and accept three classes of remote-directed operations against the installer's machine: (1) job execution — server-supplied prompts are handed to a local Codex/Claude/Mimo/OpenCode ACP runtime configured in `agent-full-access` mode with permission prompts auto-resolved by server policy, giving the remote party arbitrary command execution and file edits on the host; (2) machine-operations — the server can enumerate filesystem roots (home and `/` on POSIX, every logical drive on Windows) and list any directory including hidden entries; (3) file-requests — the server selects arbitrary paths and the client streams file contents back via POST to `/file-requests/.../stream`. The server URL is overridable via `--server` / `ACA_DEFAULT_SERVER_URL` but defaults to the author-controlled endpoint, so the default operator experience places the installer's host under remote control by whoever operates aca.kinghon.com.cn.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
